choices.text: <0.3 incompressible (Bernoulli), 0.3-0.8 subsonic compressible, 0.8-1.2 transonic (shockwaves), >1.2 supersonic, >5 hypersonic (heating critical). Physics + materials change per regime.

Compression heating: bow shock compresses air to 10,000°C+. Heat transfers via convection + radiation. Blunt body shape detaches shock further → less heat to skin (counterintuitive Apollo design lesson).

Ablative (AVCOAT, PICA-X) vaporizes carrying heat — Apollo, Dragon, Stardust. Reusable (Shuttle HRSI tiles, Starship steel) reflies but limited. Mars EDL uses backshells + heat shield + chute + Sky Crane sequence.

Thin atmosphere (~1% Earth) → less drag → high speed near surface. Heat shield (5.5 km/s → 400 m/s) → supersonic chute → rockets/Sky Crane. 7 minutes of terror; multi-stage with sequential failure points.
Propulsive landing growing — Falcon 9 booster reuse, Starship full propulsive (no chutes), Artemis Starship HLS on Moon. Precision improving 100×+ over 60 years (Mars 20 km → 7 km → <100m goal).
